summary: Added a Linux-local qd ready-queue diagnostic that separates raw exported ready status from strictly assignable qd work, reports dependency-held ready nodes with nearest blockers, and rejects requires cycles with a temp-copy smoke mode.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: just qd-ready-queue-check exists and runs with Bash/JQ only.
Current repo output explains why raw ready-status nodes are not assignable while qd ready --json is empty.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-qd-ready-queue-visibility/qd-ready-queue-check.log shows raw_ready_count=4, assignable_ready_count=0, held_ready_count=4, and qd ready --json as the authoritative assignment source; evidence/m4-qd-ready-queue-visibility/qd-ready-json.log captures the strict empty queue.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: The report identifies the M4 dependency-held chain rooted at blocked m4-product-quic-cross-device-smoke.
acceptanceEvidence.evidence: evidence/m4-qd-ready-queue-visibility/qd-ready-queue-check.log lists m4-product-quic-cross-device-smoke as the nearest blocked prerequisite and includes its blocker reason.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: A temp-copy negative smoke proves requires cycles are rejected.
Docs clarify that orchestrators choose work from strict qd ready --json, not raw exported ready status.
No product runtime, Mac hardware, workflow dispatch, cross-device validation, or PR #49 change is required or claimed.
The repository check gate passes in the Linux Nix environment.
The new Bash/JQ checker passes explicit shellcheck validation before it is tracked.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Repository qd orchestration tooling only; no product runtime, Mac hardware, workflow dispatch, cross-device validation, or PR #49 change was required or performed.
